source.title: Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage Crane - WD 32 E

actions_taken: Crane Operator extinguished fire with two hand-held fire extinguishers
BSEE follow-up inspection performed on April 2, 2014
Comprehensive repairs completed including electrical wiring replacement, hydraulic system repairs, cab refurbishment, and component replacement
BSEE approval given to return crane to service
event.summary: A fire started in the cab of an American Aero Hydraulic Crane (WD 32 E) at 22:15 hours on March 25, 2014, while the crane was unattended. The fire was caused by faulty and worn electrical wiring under the control levers floor covering that arched and created a spark, igniting oil accumulated under the cab flooring. The incident was attributed to inadequate maintenance, failure to promptly address documented crane deficiencies, and poor management of known hazards despite available inspection reports.
key_phrases: fuel source (oil) under the cab flooring
repairs not promptly taken care of
18 hours run time on some days
leaks under floor covering in cab

hazards.name: Accumulation of flammable liquids (oil) in crane cab

threats.name: Ignition of accumulated oil by electrical spark
Failure to detect and repair crane deficiencies

controls.name: Pre-use inspection of crane
controls.lod_basis: Daily inspection to identify hazards before crane operation

supporting_text: the contract Crane Operator was preparing to do the pre-use inspection for a night operation at 10:00 PM
the Crane Operator lists the deficiencies that he finds doing his pre-use inspection on the daily inspection form
he states that he feels his concerns go on deaf ears and not to operate the crane might affect his employment

controls.name: Monthly and Annual Inspection Reports
controls.barrier_role: Identification and documentation of crane deficiencies
controls.lod_basis: Systematic inspection and documentation of equipment condition

supporting_text: Review of documented and filed reports for Energy XXI, Seatrax and Black Elk, of the Pre-use Inspection Reports, Monthly Inspection Reports and Annual Inspection Reports indicate, there are and have been reports of crane deficiencies

The information concerning the hazards and unsafe conditions were available to all personnel involved in the maintenance, operation and supervision of the crane operation by reviewing the Energy XXI crane reports and past documents

controls.name: Prompt repair of critical crane components per API RP 2D
controls.barrier_role: Elimination of identified hazards through timely maintenance
controls.lod_basis: Industry standard API RP 2D requires prompt repair of critical deficiencies

supporting_text: The deficiencies were not always addressed in a manner to completely eliminate the hazard. Repairs to critical components were not promptly taken care of in accordance with API RP 2D
Records indicate that some leaks have been repaired as late as March 21, 2014. However, on the BSEE investigation on March 27, 2014, the skids were filled with oils, oily rags and absorbent pads

controls.name: Electrical wiring inspection and maintenance
controls.barrier_role: Prevention of electrical faults that could create ignition sources
controls.lod_basis: Proper electrical system maintenance prevents arcing and sparks

supporting_text: Faulty and worn electrical wiring under the control levers floor covering which arched and created a spark which ignited a fuel source (oil) under the cab flooring
There were also electrical wiring deficiencies that the Investigator observed from the battery to the starter solenoid, and to start and horn buttons and associated wiring inside the cab

controls.name: Oil containment and cleanup in crane cab

supporting_text: Reports have indicated that there were reported hydraulic hose leaks, oil leaks, damaged gauges, broken windows on cab, worn sheaves, corroded/ busted grease fittings, swing brake not working properly, leaks under floor covering in cab, leaks at controls in cab on main hoist winch
the skids were filled with oils, oily rags and absorbent pads

controls.name: Fire detection and suppression system in crane cab
controls.barrier_role: Early detection and suppression of fire
controls.lod_basis: Automatic or manual fire suppression limits fire consequences

supporting_text: he heard alarms going off from a second standby Liftboat, Triggerfish
The Crane Operator climbed up on the crane and quickly extinguished the fire with the use of two hand held fire extinguishers

controls.name: Supervision and management of crane maintenance
controls.barrier_role: Oversight of deficiency resolution and maintenance compliance
controls.lod_basis: Management oversight ensures deficiencies are addressed

supporting_text: The information concerning the hazards and unsafe conditions were available to all personnel involved in the maintenance, operation and supervision of the crane operation by reviewing the Energy XXI crane reports and past documents
he states that he feels his concerns go on deaf ears and not to operate the crane might affect his employment

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
